tweets.text: @ryangrim The government did play a big role in the history of Silicon Valley, but not in the way you imagine. A large percentage of early orders for microelectronics came from the government, for use in military aircraft, missiles, and the space program.

thread_tweets.text: @ryangrim The government did play a big role in the history of Silicon Valley, but not in the way you imagine. A large percentage of early orders for microelectronics came from the government, for use in military aircraft, missiles, and the space program.
thread_tweets.full_text: @ryangrim The government did play a big role in the history of Silicon Valley, but not in the way you imagine. A large percentage of early orders for microelectronics came from the government, for use in military aircraft, missiles, and the space program.

thread_tweets.text: @ryangrim But the fact that SV was grown on government money in that sense doesn't mean the public has some claim on it. This was sales, not subsidy. The public already got something in return for that money: a lot of chips.
thread_tweets.full_text: @ryangrim But the fact that SV was grown on government money in that sense doesn't mean the public has some claim on it. This was sales, not subsidy. The public already got something in return for that money: a lot of chips.

thread_tweets.text: @ryangrim In fact it's an important thing for governments to remember that you can't create industries in the way you seem to be imagining. Subsidies get absorbed, producing nothing. It's much more effective to place orders for things that are hard to build.
thread_tweets.full_text: @ryangrim In fact it's an important thing for governments to remember that you can't create industries in the way you seem to be imagining. Subsidies get absorbed, producing nothing. It's much more effective to place orders for things that are hard to build.

I'm frankly quite intrigued about what the future PA of $ZEC will look like

What happens most of the time when a coin pumps very hard and captures a high mindshare is that it puts a top that is very hard to break again. Then you usually get a lower high (ENA, APT), or a double https://t.co/aXB0b4SIwv

tweets.text: Very cool, former chairman and CEO of Merrill Lynch Stan O’Neal takes over as Chairman of Hut 8. It reminds me that I was actually chairman for a short  period when we started the company back in 2017. It was a very different company then, now it’s a US$13 billion decacorn!
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Today, E. Stanley (Stan) O'Neal succeeds Founding Chair Bill Tai as Chair of the Hut 8 Board of Directors.
